I couldn't find any information about the plant "Acion hookeri" in my database. However, I can suggest that you may be referring to the plant "Aconitum hookeri." 

Aconitum hookeri, also known as Hooker's monk's hood, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas. Acion hookeri has been reported to possess some therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and analgesic activities. The plant's roots, in particular, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. However, due to its toxicity, Aconitum hookeri should be handled with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional. Further research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential.
